title: journald driver: missing CONTAINER_TAG and not human readable CONTAINER_NAME
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

I've been using journald logging driver in docker where I set "--log-opt tag=" to a value containing arbitrary strings and docker variables. I tried to mimic the behavior with podman but I have very different outcome. I am not sure whether this is a bug or a missing feature.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman run --rm -ti --log-driver journald --log-opt tag="nginx|{{.ImageName}}|{{.DaemonName}}" --log-opt labels=app ubuntu:18.04 bash

2. journalctl -u machine.slice -o json | tail -n1| json_pp|less -S

**Describe the results you received:** (trimmed content to improve readability, will attach full sample)
```
{
   "_BOOT_ID" : "546ef9b86c7847b99e172835cb33011f",
   "_SYSTEMD_INVOCATION_ID" : "8f745bf862c44c1094b6a91dbafcac11",
   "CONTAINER_ID_FULL" : "04a0c89d323731b9405353fc9caa5416918e8a36301021f589c66c5133c64024",
   "__MONOTONIC_TIMESTAMP" : "371906559497",
   "_CAP_EFFECTIVE" : "3fffffffff",
   "CONTAINER_ID" : "04a0c89d3237",
   "_MACHINE_ID" : "151a80720f8fb46abd949ae05a99c4b1",
   "_GID" : "0",
   "MESSAGE" : [
      27,
      93,
      48,
      59,
      114,
      111
   ],
   "CODE_FUNC" : "write_journald",
   "_COMM" : "conmon",
   "_SOURCE_REALTIME_TIMESTAMP" : "1564173445318744",
   "_UID" : "0",
   "CONTAINER_PARTIAL_MESSAGE" : "true",
   "CONTAINER_NAME" : [
      111,
      112,
      116,
      105
   ],
   "_CMDLINE" : "/usr/lib/podman/bin/conmon -s -c 04a0c89d323731b9405353fc9caa5416918e8a36301021f589c66c5133c64024 -u 04a0c89d323731b9405353fc9caa5416918e8a36301021f589c66c5133c64024 -n optimistic_goldwasser -r /usr/bin/runc -b /var/lib/containers/storage/btrfs-containers/04a0c89d323731b9405353fc9caa5416918e8a36301021f589c66c5133c64024/userdata -p /var/run/containers/storage/btrfs-containers/04a0c89d323731b9405353fc9caa5416918e8a36301021f589c66c5133c64024/userdata/pidfile --exit-dir /var/run/libpod/exits --runtime-arg --log-format=json --runtime-arg --log --runtime-arg=/var/run/containers/storage/btrfs-containers/04a0c89d323731b9405353fc9caa5416918e8a36301021f589c66c5133c64024/userdata/oci-log --exit-command /usr/bin/podman --exit-command-arg --root --exit-command-arg /var/lib/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --runroot --exit-command-arg /var/run/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --log-level --exit-command-arg error --exit-command-arg --cgroup-manager --exit-command-arg systemd --exit-command-arg --tmpdir --exit-command-arg /var/run/libpod --exit-command-arg --runtime --exit-command-arg runc --exit-command-arg --storage-driver --exit-command-arg btrfs --exit-command-arg container --exit-command-arg cleanup --exit-command-arg --rm --exit-command-arg 04a0c89d323731b9405353fc9caa5416918e8a36301021f589c66c5133c64024 --socket-dir-path /var/run/libpod/socket -t -l journald: --log-level error",
   "_HOSTNAME" : "microchuck",
   "_PID" : "9605",
   "_EXE" : "/usr/lib/podman/bin/conmon",
   "SYSLOG_IDENTIFIER" : "conmon",
   "_SYSTEMD_SLICE" : "machine.slice",
   "CODE_FILE" : "src/ctr_logging.c",
   "CODE_LINE" : "213",
   "PRIORITY" : "6",
   "_TRANSPORT" : "journal",
   "_SYSTEMD_CGROUP" : "/machine.slice/libpod-conmon-04a0c89d323731b9405353fc9caa5416918e8a36301021f589c66c5133c64024.scope",
   "_SYSTEMD_UNIT" : "libpod-conmon-04a0c89d323731b9405353fc9caa5416918e8a36301021f589c66c5133c64024.scope",
   "__REALTIME_TIMESTAMP" : "1564173445319716",
   "__CURSOR" : "s=c252e0b60b794360b312576f0e41c3eb;i=d79edd;b=546ef9b86c7847b99e172835cb33011f;m=56975bbe09;t=58e9b82934c24;x=62364941f06c3b0b"
}
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Expect CONTAINER_TAG key to contain: "nginx|ubuntu:18.04|docker"
Expect CONTAINER_NAME to be human readable like or at least first part of ID in case it is not given.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I know variables like {{.DaemonName}} does not make sense. My actual need is to get the name of the image and its tag the container is based on. For example: "ubuntu:18.04"

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.4.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.2
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:

host:

  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0~rc6-lp151.1.2.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc6
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 2
  hostname: microchuck
  kernel: 4.12.14-lp151.28.10-default
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 103h 36m 5.02s (Approximately 4.29 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 5
  GraphDriverName: btrfs
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Build Version: 'Btrfs v4.19.1 '
    Library Version: "102"
  ImageStore:
    number: 7
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
OS is OpenSUSE Leap 15.1 amd64. Podman is from devel:kubic repository.

[journald-json-output-docker.txt](https://github.com/containers/libpod/files/3437371/journald-json-output-docker.txt)
[journald-json-output-podman.txt](https://github.com/containers/libpod/files/3437372/journald-json-output-podman.txt)

comments.body: It's both. The container name.should be human readable, but we are missing.the feature to add the image name. I'll look into.both
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
Nope, haven't found the time unfortunately
@giuseppe Any chance you could look at this?

I've opened a PR for conmon: https://github.com/containers/conmon/pull/81

That is not enough, we still need to add the Podman side
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
@giuseppe did you ever do the Podman side of this for the PR?
Thanks a lot for implementing. I am impressed.
